title: Classical Shadow Tomography
subtitle: Predicting many properties of a quantum state from few randomized measurements
description: Implementation of classical shadow tomography (Huang, Kueng, Preskill 2020): a measurement protocol that builds a compact classical estimator of an unknown quantum state from few randomized measurements, supporting efficient prediction of many observables. The notebook implements both ensembles introduced by the protocol, random-Pauli measurements (efficient for low-weight local observables, with shadow-norm scaling 3^k in the Pauli weight) and random-Clifford measurements (efficient for global observables, with shadow norm bounded by the Hilbert-Schmidt norm of the traceless part), and uses median-of-means to combine snapshots into expectation-value estimates.
